For victims in Corpus Christi, Texas, navigating the legal system while recovering from serious injuries is an overwhelming challenge that demands experienced legal guidance.

Texas law imposes a two-year statute of limitations on personal injury claims, and critical milestones like Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI) can significantly impact the value of your case. Acting quickly and strategically matters enormously.

reddit_title: Suffered a severe injury in a car accident in Texas — here's what you should know about MMI, deadlines, and finding the right attorney
reddit_body:
If you've been seriously injured in a car accident in Corpus Christi or anywhere in Texas, the medical and legal process can feel completely overwhelming. One of the most important concepts to understand is Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI)—the point at which your doctor determines your condition has stabilized. Insurance companies often use MMI as a trigger to pressure settlements, sometimes before you fully understand the long-term impact of your injuries. Don't accept any offer before reaching MMI and consulting an attorney.

Texas has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, which sounds like plenty of time but goes fast when you're focused on recovery. Evidence disappears, witnesses become harder to reach, and gaps in medical treatment can be used against you. Documenting everything—every appointment, every expense, every way the injury affects your daily life—is critical from day one.

Finding an attorney experienced specifically in severe injury cases makes a real difference in outcomes. If you're struggling to know where to start, wreckmatch.com is a free matching service that connects accident survivors with attorneys from a network of 800+ law firms based on the specifics of your case. No pressure, just a faster path to qualified legal help.
